docs.title: Steam Unlock ONENNABE
docs.text:
Welcome to the official documentation for Steam Unlock ONENNABE.

Read this guide carefully before contacting support. Most issues are covered here.

docs.title: Quick Navigation
docs.text: I want to... Go to... Install ONENNABE for the first time Installation Activate my CD Key CD Key Activation Apply bypass or online patch to a game Bypass & Online Patch Play a Denuvo game Denuvo Games Play Hypervisor game Hypervisor Tutorial Fix something that's not working Troubleshooting

docs.title: What is Steam Unlock ONENNABE?
docs.text:
Steam Unlock ONENNABE is a Windows desktop application that lets you access and download a shared library of Steam games. It works alongside your existing Steam installation.

Key highlights:

  • Browse a large catalogue of unlocked Steam games
  • Download games directly through Steam's depot system
  • Apply bypass / online patches for supported games
  • Manage your Steam library from one clean interface
  • Automatic app updates on every launch

Licensed Product

ONENNABE requires a valid CD Key purchased from an authorised seller (TikTok Shop, Shopee, or other authorised channels).

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text: Requirement Minimum Operating System Windows 10 64-bit or later RAM 4 GB (8 GB recommended) Free Disk Space 2 GB (plus space for games) Internet Stable broadband connection Steam Must be installed and logged in

docs.title: Where to Get Support
docs.text:
If this documentation does not solve your issue, join the Discord server and post in the support channel. Include:

  • Which game you are having issues with
  • Which troubleshooting steps you already tried
  • Any error messages you are seeing

docs.title: Bypass & Online Patch
docs.text: Some games in the ONENNABE catalogue support online play or have a bypass patch available. This page explains how to apply them correctly.

docs.title: Game Tags Explained
docs.text: Tag What It Means A bypass game patch, which allows game to be played An online patch for supported game, which support online play with friends, sometimes public server This game uses Hypervisor Patch, STRICTLY for really advance user, getting help from admin in discord if unsure, but please try it yourself first, tutorial is available. Requires borrowing the seller's account — see Denuvo Games

docs.title: Step-by-Step: How to Apply Bypass / Online Patch
Step 1 — Smart Apply (Tools)
docs.text:
Before downloading the game in Steam, run Smart Apply first:

  1. Open Steam Unlock ONENNABE
  2. Go to the Tools section
  3. Click Smart Apply for the game
  4. Wait for it to complete

docs.title: Step 2 — Download the Game in Steam
docs.text:
After Smart Apply finishes:

  1. Go to Steam
  2. Download and install the game as normal
  3. Wait for the download to fully complete in Steam before continuing

docs.title: Step 3 — Apply Patch from the Library Tab
docs.text:
Once the game is fully downloaded:

  1. Open Steam Unlock ONENNABE
  2. Go to the Library tab
  3. Find and click the game
  4. Click the Online Patch or Bypass Game button
  5. Wait for it to complete

That's it — your game is now patched and ready to play.

onlinefix.me pop-up

When launching a game that has Online Patch applied, a browser pop-up from onlinefix.me may appear. Close it immediately — you do not need to do anything on that site. The game will still work normally after closing the pop-up.

Note

If there is Epic Game page or Microsoft login page required and pop up upon launching the game after Online Patch, please login to enable online play. Skipping the login will disable online play.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
① Smart Apply (ONENNABE Tools)
② Download game in Steam as normal
③ Library tab in ONENNABE → Online Patch / Bypass Game button

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
• Do not skip Smart Apply. You must run it before downloading the game in Steam.
  • The game must be fully downloaded in Steam before you can apply the patch from the Library tab.
  • Antivirus must be OFF when applying the patch — bypass and online patch files will be flagged as false positives and deleted if antivirus is active. Add your Steam and ONENNABE folders to exclusions (see Installation).

docs.title: Still Not Working?
docs.text: See the Troubleshooting page for step-by-step fixes.

docs.title: Cannot Connect to Update Server
docs.text:
If ONENNABE shows "Could not connect to update server." on the splash screen with an OFF VPN/WARP AND RETRY button, the app is unable to reach the update server during startup.

Follow the fixes below in order until the issue is resolved.

docs.title: Fix 1 — Update ONENNABE to the Latest Version
docs.text:
You may be running an outdated version of ONENNABE that can no longer reach the update server.

  1. Download the latest ONENNABE installer from the link provided by your seller
  2. Run the installer over your existing installation — your activation and games are kept
  3. Relaunch ONENNABE and check if the error is gone

docs.title: Fix 2 — Check Your System Date & Time
docs.text:
An incorrect system date or time will cause SSL certificate validation to fail, which prevents ONENNABE from connecting to any server.

  1. Right-click the clock in the bottom-right corner of your taskbar
  2. Click Adjust date/time
  3. Make sure Set time automatically is toggled ON
  4. Click Sync now to force an immediate time sync
  5. Relaunch ONENNABE

Warning

Even being a few minutes off can cause connection failures. Always use automatic time sync.

docs.title: Fix 3 — Turn Off VPN, Cloudflare WARP, or Custom DNS
docs.text: VPNs, Cloudflare WARP, and custom DNS settings can block or redirect ONENNABE's connection to the update server.

docs.title: Turn Off VPN
docs.text:
1. Open your VPN app
  2. Disconnect or turn it off completely
  3. Click OFF VPN/WARP AND RETRY in ONENNABE, or relaunch the app

docs.title: Turn Off Cloudflare WARP
docs.text:
1. Click the WARP icon in your system tray
  2. Click the toggle to turn WARP off
  3. Click OFF VPN/WARP AND RETRY in ONENNABE, or relaunch the app

docs.title: Reset DNS to Default
docs.text:
If you are using a custom DNS (e.g. 1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8, or any manually set DNS):

  1. Open Settings → Network & Internet → Wi-Fi or Ethernet (depending on your connection)
  2. Click your active connection → Edit under DNS settings
  3. Set DNS to Automatic (DHCP)
  4. Save and relaunch ONENNABE

Tip

The OFF VPN/WARP AND RETRY button on the splash screen is a quick shortcut — after turning off your VPN or WARP, click it to retry the connection without fully closing and reopening the app.

docs.title: Still Not Working?
docs.text:
If all three fixes have been tried and the error persists, join the Discord server and post in the support channel. Include:

  • Your ONENNABE version number
  • Your Windows version
  • Whether you are using a VPN, WARP, or custom DNS

docs.title: CD Key Activation
docs.text: Your CD Key is what activates ONENNABE and gives you access to the game catalogue. You receive this from your seller after purchase.

docs.title: How to Activate
docs.text:
1. Make sure Steam is running and you are logged in
  2. Open Steam Unlock ONENNABE
  3. The activation screen will appear automatically on first launch
  4. Enter your CD Key exactly as provided — it is case-sensitive
  5. Click Activate
  6. Wait for the key to be verified
  7. Your game library will appear after successful activation

docs.title: Where to Get Your CD Key
docs.text:
Purchase Platform How to Get Key TikTok Shop Wait for parcel to arrive, then DM the seller Shopee Chat with seller in Shopee chat Other channels Contact your seller directly

TikTok Shop Buyers

Your parcel must arrive before the seller will provide your CD Key. Wait for the delivery notification from TikTok, then DM the seller to request your key.

docs.title: Key Rules
docs.text:
• One key = one Steam account. Your key is tied to the Steam account you activate it on.
  • Never share your key. Sharing it will cause it to be deactivated.
  • Keys are case-sensitive. Copy and paste the key if possible to avoid typos.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
"License invalid" error Double-check the key for typos. Make sure Steam is running and you are online. If copied from a message, check for extra spaces before or after the key.

Key was working before but now shows invalid Restart Steam and reopen ONENNABE. If the issue persists, contact your seller.

Key not provided by seller yet If you bought via TikTok Shop, the parcel must arrive first. For Shopee or other platforms, DM your seller directly in the purchase chat.

Activated on wrong Steam account Contact your seller to request a key transfer.

docs.title: Cloud Save Settings
docs.text:
Steam Unlock ONENNABE has a built-in Cloud Save system that backs up your game list, achievements, and save files to Google Drive.

Access it by clicking ☁️ Cloud Save Settings in the top navigation bar.

ONENNABE must be running

Steam Unlock ONENNABE must be open and running at all times for cloud saves to work. Auto-backup on startup, push, and pull all require the app to be active.

docs.title: Setting Up — Connect Google Drive
docs.text:
Before using any cloud save features, you must connect your Google Drive account.

  1. Click ☁️ Cloud Save Settings in the top navbar
  2. Click ☁️ Connect Google Drive
  3. A browser window will open asking you to sign in to Google and grant permission
  4. Complete the sign-in in the browser
  5. The status will change to Connected (green dot) once done

To disconnect, click Disconnect Google Drive. Your files on Drive are kept — only the sync connection is removed.

docs.title: Section 1 — Cloud Sync (Game List Backup)
docs.text: Backs up your unlocked game list to Google Drive so you can restore it on another PC or after a reinstall.

docs.title: Auto-backup Game List
docs.text: Toggle ON to automatically back up your game list to Google Drive every time ONENNABE starts.

docs.title: Backup Now
docs.text: Manually triggers an immediate upload of your current game list to Google Drive.

docs.title: Restore from Drive (Merge)
docs.text: Downloads your backed-up game list from Google Drive and merges it with your current list — games already on this PC are kept, and any missing games from Drive are added back.

docs.title: Replace All
docs.text:
Fully replaces your current game list with the one stored on Google Drive. Use this when setting up on a new PC and you want an exact copy of your previous setup.

Note

Last backup and Last restore timestamps are shown below the buttons so you always know when your list was last synced.

docs.title: Section 2 — Save Games Backup
docs.text:
Backs up your actual game save files to Google Drive. This works for:

  • Standard Steam save games
  • Online Fix save games (games using onlinefix patches)
  • Cracked game save files

Supported save locations

ONENNABE scans known save file locations per game AppID. Not all games store saves in detectable locations — if a game's saves are not found, they will be marked as skipped during backup.

docs.title: Auto-backup Save Games
docs.text: Toggle ON to automatically back up all detected save files to Google Drive every time ONENNABE starts.

docs.title: Backup Now (Force Backup)
docs.text:
Manually triggers an immediate backup of all save files for every installed game. A progress log window will appear showing:

  • ✅ Pushed — save files successfully uploaded to Google Drive
  • ⏭ Skipped — save files already up to date on Drive, no changes detected
  • ❌ Errors — save files that could not be uploaded

docs.title: Restore Save Games from Drive
docs.text:
Downloads your backed-up save files from Google Drive for every installed game on this PC.

  • Newer local saves are always kept — only older files are overwritten with the Drive copy
  • Close any running games before restoring to avoid file conflicts

Switching PCs

To move your saves to a new PC: back up on your old PC → install ONENNABE on the new PC → connect the same Google account → click Restore Save Games from Drive.

docs.title: What Gets Backed Up
docs.text: Feature What Is Backed Up Game List Backup Your unlocked game list (which games are in your library) Save Games Backup Save files for Steam games, Online Fix games, and cracked games

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
• ONENNABE must be open for auto-backup to trigger on startup — if the app is closed, no automatic backup happens
  • Google Drive storage counts against your Google account's free quota (15 GB). Save files for many games can add up over time
  • Achievements are tied to your Steam account and are not backed up here — they sync via Steam's own servers
  • Disconnecting Google Drive does not delete your files on Drive — you can reconnect at any time to resume syncing

docs.title: 🛠️ Step 1: Initial Configuration
docs.text:
1. Open CloudRedirect.
  2. Go to Mode and click "I understand the risk".
  3. Select CloudRedirect Mode.
  4. Click Setup ➜ Run all patch.

Troubleshooting

If you are unable to click the patch button, go to [SUO > Tools > Reinstall Steam], then attempt to run the patch again.

  1. Click Continue and wait for the process to finish.
  2. Click Configure Provider and choose either Google Drive or OneDrive.
  3. Login in the browser window that opens and click Allow.
  4. Click Save Configuration and Restart Steam.

Cloud Save Integration

  • Steps 1–5 are necessary to pin the manifest.
  • Steps 6–8 are optional and only required if you wish to enable cloud saves.

docs.title: 🔒 Step 2: How to Lock Game Versions
docs.text:
Purpose

Use this feature to prevent Steam from automatically updating your games.

  1. Click on Manifest Pinning.
  2. Select Enable Manifest Pinning.
  3. Find your game name or App ID in the LUA app list.
  4. Enable the toggle to lock that specific version.
  5. Click Restart Steam.

docs.title: Denuvo Games — Borrowing Account
docs.text:
Denuvo is a DRM (Digital Rights Management) system used by some games. These games require an extra step — you must borrow the seller's Steam account to activate the Denuvo licence on your machine before you can play from your own account.

Danger

Denuvo games will NOT work without borrowing an account from your seller. You cannot skip this step.

docs.title: How to Identify a Denuvo Game
docs.text: In the ONENNABE Store tab, Denuvo games will be tagged with the Denuvo label on the game card.

docs.title: Step-by-Step: Playing a Denuvo Game
docs.text:
1. Find the game in the Store tab — confirm it has the Denuvo tag
  2. Click the game card → click Enable Auto Update in ONENNABE
  3. Go to Steam and download the game normally
  4. Close Steam Unlock ONENNABE — do this before the next step
  5. Contact your seller and ask to borrow the account for your game
  6. Switch Steam to the seller's account using the credentials they provide
  7. Launch the game and play until you reach the main menu
  8. Switch Steam back to your own account
  9. Launch and play from your own account — enjoy!

docs.title: Important Rules
docs.text:
Close ONENNABE First

You must close ONENNABE before switching to the seller's account. If ONENNABE is still open, the process may not work correctly.

  • You must reach the main menu on the seller's account — just launching the game is not enough. Denuvo requires a brief online validation session.
  • Switch back to your own account promptly after reaching the main menu. Do not use the seller's account for anything else.
  • Each account borrow has a session limit. Only use it to launch the game to the main menu.

docs.title: Exception — Resident Evil Requiem
docs.text:
Special Case

Resident Evil Requiem cannot be played by switching back to your own account. This game must be played entirely from the seller's account in Steam Offline Mode. Do NOT switch back to your own account for this title. Ask your seller for specific instructions.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Game says "not available" after switching back to my account Make sure you reached the main menu on the seller's account before switching back. If you did, restart Steam and try again.

Game crashes or shows a Denuvo error The Denuvo session may have expired. Contact your seller to borrow the account again.

Seller's account credentials not working Contact your seller directly via the platform you purchased from (TikTok/Shopee chat or Discord DM).

I forgot to close ONENNABE before switching accounts Close ONENNABE now, switch to the seller's account, launch to main menu, switch back, then reopen ONENNABE.

docs.title: Still Not Working?
docs.text: See the Troubleshooting page for step-by-step fixes.

docs.title: Downloading ONENNABE
docs.text: Before downloading, you must turn off Real-Time Protection in Windows Defender. If you skip this step, the installer will be deleted automatically as soon as it finishes downloading.

docs.title: Step 1 — Turn Off Real-Time Protection
docs.text:
🛡️ Open Real-Time Protection Settings Note: Click "Allow" or "Open Windows Security" if your browser prompts you.

  1. Open Windows Security (search for it in the Start menu)
  2. Click Virus & threat protection
  3. Click Manage settings under "Virus & threat protection settings"
  4. Toggle Real-time protection to Off

Warning

Keep Real-Time Protection off until after ONENNABE is fully installed. You can turn it back on once installation is complete, but make sure you have added the exclusion folders first — see Installation.

docs.title: Step 2 — Download via Google Chrome
docs.text:
or this

Watch the video below for a full walkthrough of the download process:

docs.title: Chrome Download Steps
docs.text:
1. Open Google Chrome
  2. Go to the ONENNABE download link provided by your seller
  3. Click the download button
  4. If Chrome shows a warning saying "This file may be harmful": a. 1st Method
    • Click the arrow (⌄) next to the discard button
    • Click Keep or Keep anyway b. 2nd Method
    • On your Keyboard, press CTRL + J
    • It will open download page
    • Find the file
    • Click the three dots (⋮) next to it
    • Click “Keep” → then “Keep anyway”
  5. Wait for the download to complete
  6. Open the downloaded installer

Why Chrome?

Chrome handles the "Keep anyway" prompt more reliably than other browsers for unsigned executables. Edge may block the file entirely without an option to keep it.

docs.title: Step 3 — Install ONENNABE
docs.text: Once downloaded, proceed to the Installation Guide to complete the setup.

docs.title: Hypervisor Guide
docs.text: This guide covers everything you need to set up and run Hypervisor (HV) games through Steam Unlock ONENNABE.

docs.title: 1 — Disable Windows Hello
docs.text: Windows Hello can interfere with the Hypervisor setup. Disable it before proceeding.

docs.title: 2 — Download HV Tools
docs.text:
Download HV Tools V3.4 — this tool checks your system and shows exactly what has been done and what still needs to be configured.

Download HV Tools V3.4

docs.title: 3 — Enable Virtualization in BIOS
docs.text:
Virtualization must be enabled in your BIOS. Follow the video guide for your motherboard brand below.

Additional BIOS settings

  • Secure Boot — sometimes needs to be turned off
  • Test Signing Mode — sometimes needs to be turned on

HV Tools will tell you which of these apply to your system.

Select your motherboard brand:

Brand Video Guide MSI Watch Guide Gigabyte Watch Guide ASRock Watch Guide ASUS Watch Guide Dell Watch Guide Lenovo Watch Guide

docs.title: Verify Virtualization is Enabled
docs.text:
After enabling in BIOS, confirm it is active:

  1. Press Ctrl + Shift + Esc to open Task Manager
  2. Click the Performance tab
  3. Click CPU
  4. Look for Virtualization: Enabled at the bottom right

docs.title: 4 — Lock Game Version in ONENNABE
docs.text:
Before downloading any game from Steam, you must lock the game version in ONENNABE first:

SUO > Tools > Game Auto Update > click Open > Click Smart Apply

Default state

By default, games are in Lock mode. Disable = Lock | Enable = Unlock Leave it as Lock (Disable) before downloading.

docs.title: Bypass Hypervisor Guide
docs.text: Once prerequisites are complete, follow these steps to bypass:

docs.title: Step 1 — Turn Off Memory Integrity
docs.text:
1. Open Windows Security
  2. Go to Device Security → Core Isolation
  3. Turn off Memory Integrity

Note

Memory Integrity is off by default on most systems. If it is already off, skip this step.

docs.title: Step 2 — Turn Off Antivirus
docs.text:
Disable Real-time Protection in Windows Defender (or your third-party antivirus) before downloading.

See Installation for how to add exclusions so you don't need to turn it off every time.

docs.title: Step 3 — Download the Game from Steam
docs.text: With the game version locked and antivirus off, download the game normally from Steam. Wait for it to fully complete.

docs.title: Step 4 — Apply Bypass
docs.text:
1. Open Steam Unlock ONENNABE
  2. Go to the Library tab
  3. Find the game
  4. Click Bypass Game

docs.title: Launch Options
docs.text: After bypass is applied, you have three ways to launch the game:

docs.title: Option 1 — Launch from ONENNABE (Recommended)
docs.text:
1. Open Steam Unlock ONENNABE
  2. Go to the Library tab
  3. Click the game → click Play
  4. Choose the .exe file or choose HV-PlugNPlay.bat / HV-Launcher.bat

Windows 11 Only

HV-PlugNPlay.bat and HV-Launcher.bat only work on Windows 11.

docs.title: Option 2 — Launch Directly from File
docs.text:
Navigate to the game's install folder and run:

  • HV-PlugNPlay.bat, or
  • HV-Launcher.bat, or
  • [GAMENAME].exe

docs.title: Option 3 — Launch from Steam (with HV Launch Option)
docs.text:
To launch from Steam directly, you must first inject the HV launch option via ONENNABE:

SUO > Tools > Inject HV Launch Option

After injecting, you can launch the game normally from Steam.

docs.title: Still Not Working?
docs.text: See the Troubleshooting page for step-by-step fixes.

docs.title: Installation Guide
docs.text: Complete this page top to bottom before launching ONENNABE for the first time.

docs.title: Step 1 — Prepare Windows Security
docs.text: ONENNABE modifies Steam configuration files to unlock games. Windows and antivirus programs treat this as suspicious (false positive). You must configure security settings before installing, or the app files will be deleted.

docs.title: 1A — Turn Off Smart App Control (Windows 11 only)
docs.text:
⚙️ Open Smart App Control Note: Click "Allow" or "Open Settings" if your browser prompts you.

  1. Open Settings → Privacy & Security → Windows Security
  2. Click App & Browser Control
  3. Click Smart App Control settings
  4. Set to Off

Warning

If Smart App Control is set to On or Evaluating, ONENNABE will be blocked. There is no workaround other than turning it off.

docs.title: 1B — Add Windows Defender Exclusions
docs.text:
🛡️ Open Exclusions Settings Note: Click "Allow" or "Open Windows Security" if your browser prompts you.

  1. Open Settings → Privacy & Security → Windows Security
  2. Click Virus & threat protection
  3. Click Manage settings (under Virus & threat protection settings)
  4. Scroll to Exclusions → click Add or remove exclusions
  5. Click Add an exclusion → Folder — add all three:

C:\Program Files\Steam

C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam

C:\Program Files\Steam Unlock ONENNABE

Third-party antivirus

If you use Avast, Kaspersky, Norton, Bitdefender, or any other antivirus, you must add the same folder exclusions inside that antivirus app as well.

docs.title: Step 2 — Install Steam
docs.text:
If Steam is not already installed:

  1. Download Steam from store.steampowered.com
  2. Install and log in to your personal Steam account
  3. Keep Steam running — ONENNABE requires an active Steam session

docs.title: Step 3 — Install ONENNABE
docs.text:
1. Download the ONENNABE installer from the link your seller provided
  2. Right-click the file → Run as Administrator
  3. If Windows SmartScreen shows a blue popup saying "Windows protected your PC":
    • Click "More info"
    • Click "Run anyway"
  4. Follow the on-screen installer prompts
  5. Do not change the default installation path
  6. Launch ONENNABE from the desktop shortcut when done

Default installation path

C:\Program Files\Steam Unlock ONENNABE\

docs.title: Step 4 — Activate Your CD Key
docs.text: See CD Key Activation to complete setup.

docs.title: Installation Order Summary
docs.text:
① Windows security setup (exclusions + Smart App Control off)
② Install Steam + log in
③ Install ONENNABE
④ Activate CD Key

docs.title: Common Installation Issues
docs.text:
Installer gets deleted after download Your antivirus removed it. Complete Step 1B first, then re-download.

"Windows protected your PC" blue popup Click "More info" → "Run anyway". This is a SmartScreen false positive.

App won't open after install Right-click the shortcut → Run as Administrator. Also make sure Steam is running.

Black screen / WebView2 error on launch Install Microsoft Edge WebView2 Runtime — this powers the app interface.

docs.title: Removing Games from the Library
docs.text:
The Library tab lets you remove one or multiple games at once using the checkbox selector and the 🗑️ trash button.

What does removing do?

Removing a game deletes its unlock file from your library. This removes the game from Steam's library view. It does not uninstall the game files from your PC — your downloaded game data in steamapps is kept.

docs.title: Step 1 — Select Games Using the Checkbox
docs.text:
Each game in the left sidebar has a checkbox (✓) on its left side.

  1. Go to the Library tab
  2. Click the ✓ checkbox on the left of any game you want to remove
  3. The checkbox turns highlighted to show it is selected
  4. Repeat for every game you want to remove
  5. You can select as many games as you want

Tip

Clicking the game row itself opens the game detail panel. To select a game for removal, click specifically on the ✓ checkbox, not the game name or icon.

docs.title: Step 2 — Click the 🗑️ Trash Button
docs.text:
Once at least one game is selected, the 🗑️ trash button will appear next to the search bar at the top of the library sidebar.

  1. Click the 🗑️ trash button
  2. A confirmation dialog will appear showing how many games will be removed
  3. Click OK / Confirm to proceed
  4. The selected games are removed from your library

A success notification will appear confirming how many games were removed.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
① Click ✓ checkbox on each game you want to remove
② 🗑️ trash button appears in the search bar area
③ Click 🗑️ → confirm the dialog
④ Games are removed from the library

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
• Removing does not uninstall. Your game files remain on disk in steamapps. Only the unlock file is deleted.
  • To re-add a removed game, go back to the Store tab, find the game, and unlock it again.
  • The trash button only appears when at least one game is selected. If you do not see it, click a checkbox first.
  • To deselect a game, click its checkbox again — the highlight will be removed and the game will no longer be included in the removal.

docs.title: Still Not Working?
docs.text: See the Troubleshooting page for step-by-step fixes.

docs.title: Library Tab
docs.text: The Library tab shows all games that have been unlocked on your Steam account via ONENNABE.

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
• Left sidebar — lists all your unlocked games, searchable by name
  • Right panel — shows the selected game's details, cover art, and action buttons

docs.title: Finding a Game
docs.text: Use the search box at the top of the left sidebar to filter by name. Click any game to load its detail panel on the right.

docs.title: Right-Click Context Menu
docs.text: Right-clicking a game in the Steam Library opens Steam's context menu with these options:

docs.title: ▶ Play
docs.text: Launches the game directly. This is the standard way to start a game from Steam.

docs.title: Add to Favorites
docs.text: Marks the game as a favourite in your Steam library for quick access.

docs.title: Browse Local Files
docs.text:
Opens the game's installation folder in Windows Explorer. Useful for:

  • Manually running .bat or .exe files (e.g. HV-PlugNPlay.bat, HV-Launcher.bat)
  • Checking if bypass or patch files are present
  • Troubleshooting missing files

docs.title: Verify Integrity of Game Files
docs.text:
Steam checks all game files and re-downloads any that are missing or corrupted.

Warning

Do not use Verify Integrity after applying a bypass or online patch. Steam will detect the patched files as modified and replace them with the original versions, removing your patch. You will need to re-apply the patch afterwards.

docs.title: Uninstall
docs.text:
Uninstalls the game from your PC. The game will remain in your Steam library and can be re-downloaded at any time.

Note

Uninstalling does not remove the game from ONENNABE. Your unlock is still active — just re-download and re-apply the patch if needed.

docs.title: Secondary Action Buttons
docs.text:
When you select a game in the Library tab, a secondary action bar appears below the main Play button. These buttons provide additional tools for managing the game's patches and DRM.

Visibility rules

Patch Standalone, Steamless, and Restore Original are hidden for games that use Bypass, Online Patch, or Hypervisor methods — those games use their own patching system and do not need these tools.

docs.title: 🗑️ Remove Hypervisor Patch
docs.text:
Appears on games that have a Hypervisor Bypass or Bypass applied.

What it does: - Removes all Hypervisor bypass files from the game folder - Triggers Steam to validate and restore the original game files

When to use: - You want to revert the game back to its original unpatched state - The Hypervisor patch is causing issues and you want a clean slate before re-applying

Warning

After removing the Hypervisor patch, you will need to re-apply it from the Library tab before the game will work again. See Hypervisor Games for how to re-apply.

docs.title: 🩹 Patch Standalone / 🗑️ Remove Patch Standalone
docs.text:
This button toggles based on whether the standalone patch (onennabe_launcher.exe) is currently installed in the game folder.

State Button Shown What It Does Not installed 🩹 Patch Standalone Installs the standalone launcher into the game folder Already installed 🗑️ Remove Patch Standalone Removes the standalone launcher from the game folder

When to use Patch Standalone: - The game needs a standalone launcher to run outside of Steam, this allows game to run without steam at all, but this doesn't work with all games. - You are setting up a game that requires the cold client method

When to use Remove Patch Standalone: - You want to revert the game to its original state - The standalone patch is causing launch issues

Note

Patch Standalone and Remove Patch Standalone are hidden for games that use Bypass, Online Patch, or Hypervisor — those methods do not use the standalone launcher.

docs.title: 🛡 Steamless [Remove Steam DRM]
docs.text:
Runs Steamless — a tool that strips Steam DRM from the game's executable files. This allows the game executable to run without Steam's DRM layer.

What it does: - Scans the game folder for DRM-protected executables - Replaces them with unpacked, DRM-free versions - The original files are backed up with a .BAK extension

When to use: - A game fails to launch and DRM is suspected as the cause, this usually solve error 54 and error 6005432. Unfortunately this doesn't work with all games, especially games with Enigma Protector. - You need to run the game executable directly without Steam

Warning

Only use Steamless if you know the game needs it. Running it on games that don't need it may cause launch issues.

docs.title: ↩️ Restore Original [Undo Steamless]
docs.text:
Reverts the Steamless operation by restoring the original .BAK backup files.

What it does: - Deletes the Steamless-unpacked executable - Restores the original game executable from the .BAK backup

When to use: - Steamless caused the game to break or not launch correctly - You want to go back to the original DRM-protected executable

Note

This button only works if a .BAK backup file exists — meaning Steamless was previously run on the game. If no backup exists, this button will have no effect.

docs.title: Enable Online Function Toggle
docs.text:
When you select a game in the Library tab, some games will show an Enable Online Function toggle in the game detail panel.

Availability

This toggle is only shown for games that do not use Denuvo, Bypass, or Hypervisor methods. Games using those methods have their own online handling and do not use this toggle.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
Enabling this toggle adds -onlinefix to the game's Steam launch options. This enables online functionality for games that use:

  • Steam P2P (peer-to-peer) servers
  • Epic Online Services (EOS) P2P servers

Steam will close and restart automatically when you toggle this on or off to apply the change.

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text:
1. Select a game in the Library tab
  2. Find the Enable Online Function toggle in the game detail panel
  3. Toggle it ON
  4. A confirmation dialog will appear — click OK
  5. Steam will close and restart to apply the launch option
  6. Launch the game and try playing online

To disable it, toggle it OFF and confirm — Steam will restart again to remove the launch option.

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
Experimental — does not work with all games

Enable Online Function works with games that use Steam P2P or Epic Online Services P2P networking. It does not work with games that use dedicated servers, other DRM-protected online systems, or custom matchmaking solutions.

  • If online play still does not work after enabling this, the game likely uses a server type that is not supported — check Discord for game-specific instructions

docs.title: Removing Games from the Library
docs.text: See Removing Games from the Library for how to use the checkbox selector and 🗑️ trash button to remove one or more games from your library.

docs.title: Still Not Working?
docs.text: See the Troubleshooting page for step-by-step fixes.

docs.title: OpenSteamTool — Unsupported Version & IPC Errors
docs.text: If you see any of the following popups when launching ONENNABE or Steam, it means there is a version mismatch between OpenSteamTool and your current Steam installation.

docs.title: The Errors
docs.text:
These three popups are all related to the same root cause:

Error Message Unsupported Steam Version (steamui.dll) signature file not found for steamui.dll Unsupported Steam Version (steamclient64.dll) signature file not found for steamclient64.dll IPC spec missing IPC spec file not found

These are warnings, not crashes

These popups are informational. The affected hooks are disabled for the current session but other modules continue to work. Click OK to dismiss and continue.

docs.title: Why This Happens
docs.text:
OpenSteamTool hooks into specific Steam DLL files (steamui.dll, steamclient64.dll) using pattern signatures. When Steam updates to a new version, the signatures change and OpenSteamTool's upstream needs to be updated to match.

There are two possible causes:

docs.title: Cause 1 — OpenSteamTool Upstream Not Yet Updated
docs.text:
Steam just pushed a new update and the OpenSteamTool upstream has not caught up yet.

Fix: Wait 1–2 hours

Please be patient

The OpenSteamTool upstream is maintained separately. After a Steam update, it usually takes 1–2 hours for the upstream to be updated with new signatures. Once the upstream is updated, the error will disappear automatically on the next Steam restart — no action needed on your end.

What to do: 1. Click OK to dismiss the popup 2. Wait 1–2 hours 3. Restart Steam 4. If the error is gone, you are good to go 5. If the error persists after 2+ hours, move to Cause 2 below

docs.title: Cause 2 — Your Steam is Outdated
docs.text:
The OpenSteamTool upstream is already updated to the latest Steam version, but your Steam installation is behind and its DLL signatures do not match.

This can happen if: - Steam failed to auto-update - A steam.cfg file in your Steam folder is blocking updates - Your Steam install is corrupted

Fix — Reinstall Steam for a Fresh Update

The cleanest solution is to reinstall Steam using the built-in ONENNABE tool, which ensures you get a completely fresh Steam installation without a steam.cfg block.

  1. Open Steam Unlock ONENNABE
  2. Go to Tools
  3. Click Reinstall Steam
  4. Wait for the reinstall to complete
  5. Log back in to Steam
  6. Relaunch ONENNABE

See Fix 4 — Reinstall Steam for the full step-by-step guide.

Check for steam.cfg

Before reinstalling, check if a file named steam.cfg exists in your Steam installation folder (usually C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\ or C:\Program Files\Steam\). If it exists, delete it — this file can block Steam from updating to the latest version. Reinstalling Steam via ONENNABE Tools will handle this automatically.

docs.title: Quick Decision Guide
docs.text:
Seeing OST error popup?
│
├── Did Steam just update recently? (within last 1–2 hours)
│   └── YES → Wait 1–2 hours, restart Steam, check again
│
└── Error still showing after 2+ hours?
    └── Reinstall Steam via ONENNABE Tools → Tools > Reinstall Steam

docs.title: Still Not Working?
docs.text: If the error persists after both fixes, join the Discord server and post in the support channel with a screenshot of the error popup. Include your Steam build ID shown in the popup diagnostics section.

docs.title: Using the PLAY Button
docs.text: The ▶ PLAY button in the Library tab is the main way to launch your games. Clicking it opens a small dropdown menu with three options depending on how the game was installed.

docs.title: How to Open the Play Menu
docs.text:
1. Go to the Library tab in ONENNABE
  2. Click a game in the left sidebar to open its detail panel
  3. Click the ▶ PLAY button
  4. A dropdown menu appears with your launch options

docs.title: ▶ Play from Steam
docs.text:
Launches the game directly through Steam — the same as clicking Play in Steam itself.

When to use: - Standard games with no bypass or patch - Denuvo games (after completing the account borrow step) - Any game where you have already applied the necessary patches

docs.title: 📁 Choose .exe or .bat
docs.text:
Opens a file picker so you can select the exact executable or batch file to launch.

When to use: - Hypervisor games — select HV-PlugNPlay.bat or HV-Launcher.bat - Games with multiple launch executables where you need to pick a specific one - Bypass or Online Patch games where a specific launcher is required

Windows 11 Only

HV-PlugNPlay.bat and HV-Launcher.bat only work on Windows 11. These files will not work on Windows 10.

How to use: 1. Click ▶ PLAY 2. Select 📁 Choose .exe or .bat 3. A Windows file browser opens in the game's installation folder 4. Select the correct file: - HV-PlugNPlay.bat or HV-Launcher.bat — for Hypervisor games - [GameName].exe — for games that need to be launched directly 5. Click Open — the game will launch

docs.title: 🗑 Uninstall Game
docs.text:
Opens Steam's native uninstall dialog for the game.

What it does: - Triggers Steam to uninstall the game files from your PC - Your unlock in ONENNABE remains active — you can re-download the game at any time - The game stays in your Steam library after uninstalling

Note

If the game was installed via Download Standalone instead of Steam, this option will show as 🗑 Uninstall Download instead, which permanently deletes the game folder and JSON file directly.

docs.title: Button States
docs.text:
The PLAY button changes depending on the game's current status:

Button Label What It Means ▶ PLAY Game is installed and ready to launch ↓ INSTALL Game is not yet downloaded — click to start installing via Steam INSTALLING… X% Game is currently downloading in Steam — wait for it to finish LOADING… ONENNABE is checking the game's install status

docs.title: Still Not Working?
docs.text: See the Troubleshooting page for step-by-step fixes.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: Access the Settings panel by clicking ⚙ Settings in the top navigation bar.

docs.title: 🔑 Activate App
docs.text:
Opens the CD Key activation screen. Use this if your activation has expired or you need to re-enter your key after a reinstall.

See CD Key Activation for full details.

docs.title: 🗑️ Reset Activation
docs.text:
Removes the currently bound CD Key and Steam account binding from this installation. Use this only if you need to re-activate on a different Steam account and already obtain new CDKEY from your seller.

Danger

Resetting activation will NOT unbind your CD Key from SteamID. Contact your seller before doing this.

docs.title: Parental Control
docs.text:
Hides adult content from the Store tab.

When enabled, a 4–8 digit PIN is required to turn it off again.

docs.title: How to Enable
docs.text:
1. Open ⚙ Settings
  2. Toggle Parental Control to ON
  3. A PIN setup dialog will appear
  4. Enter a 4–8 digit PIN
  5. Confirm the PIN
  6. Click OK

Adult content is now hidden from the Store tab.

docs.title: How to Disable
docs.text:
1. Open ⚙ Settings
  2. Toggle Parental Control to OFF
  3. Enter your PIN when prompted
  4. Click OK

Do not forget your PIN

There is no PIN recovery option. If you forget your PIN, contact support.

docs.title: OpenSteamTool
docs.text:
Enables or disables the OpenSteamTool.dll — the core unlocker that hooks into Steam to apply game unlocks.

State What Happens ON (default) OpenSteamTool is active — games are unlocked via the DLL hook OFF OpenSteamTool is disabled — reverts to using Steam's built-in tools

When to turn this OFF

Turn off OpenSteamTool if:

  • Your Denuvo borrow account is not working
  • You are experiencing Steam login issues caused by the hook

Steam must be restarted after toggling for the change to take effect.

docs.title: How to Toggle
docs.text:
1. Open ⚙ Settings
  2. Find OpenSteamTool under the Settings section
  3. Toggle it ON or OFF
  4. Restart Steam to apply the change

docs.title: Resize UI
docs.text:
Adjusts the zoom level of the ONENNABE interface.

  • Press ＋ to zoom in
  • Press − to zoom out

Useful if the interface appears too small or too large on your display.

docs.title: Minimize to System Tray
docs.text:
When enabled, closing the ONENNABE window keeps the app running in the system tray (bottom-right corner of the taskbar) instead of fully exiting.

  • Right-click the tray icon to open or exit the app
  • Useful if you want ONENNABE to stay running in the background

docs.title: Always Ask Before Closing
docs.text:
When enabled, ONENNABE will always ask whether to minimize to tray or quit when you close the window.

When disabled, ONENNABE remembers your last choice and applies it automatically without asking.

docs.title: Latest Steam Client Versions
docs.text:
This table updates automatically every 6 hours.

Channel Version Updated (UTC) Stable 1782866176 2026-07-01 00:36:16 UTC Beta 1784045513 2026-07-14 16:11:53 UTC

docs.title: Tools
docs.text: The Tools section in ONENNABE provides utilities to manage your games, fix Steam issues, and maintain your setup. Access it by clicking Tools in the top navigation bar.

docs.title: 🔄 Game Auto Update
docs.text:
Manages the auto-update setting per game in Steam.

  • Enable (Auto Update) — Steam will automatically update the game when a new version is available
  • Lock — pins the game to its current version and prevents Steam from updating it

docs.title: Smart Apply
docs.text:
Click Smart Apply inside Game Auto Update to automatically apply the recommended setting to all your games at once:

Game Type Recommended Setting Denuvo Protected Auto Update No special tags Auto Update Hypervisor Bypass Lock Bypass Available Lock

Tip

Always run Smart Apply before downloading a Bypass, Online Patch, or Hypervisor game to make sure the version is locked correctly.

docs.title: 🧹 Clear Steam Download Cache
docs.text:
Clears Steam's download cache and temporary files, then restarts Steam automatically.

Use this when: - Downloads are corrupted or failing - Steam is stuck in an update loop - Patching is unusually slow - Re deploy Steam Unlock unlocker files

docs.title: 🩹 Fix Unlock Game Issue
docs.text:
Clears Steam's config folder to fix games that won't unlock or throw config errors. This is an attempt to fix unlocking game issue

What is kept: - avatarcache - depotcache - stplug-in (your unlocked games)

Steam closes and restarts automatically after the fix.

You will need to sign in to Steam again

Your unlocked games are safe, but your Steam login session will be cleared.

docs.title: 🔄 Reinstall Steam
docs.text:
Downloads a fresh Steam installer and performs a clean reinstall of Steam. This mostly fix issue game is not being unlock despite already clicking unlock game You will have to restart Steam Unlock too after reinstalling fresh Steam.

What is kept: - Your unlocked games (stplug-in) - Your installed games (steamapps)

What is deleted: - Steam login session - userdata/ folder - All other Steam configuration files

The unlock hook is re-deployed automatically after the reinstall.

You will need to sign in to Steam again

Have your Steam username and password ready before running this. Everything except your games and unlocks will be wiped.

docs.title: 💉 Inject HV Launch Options
docs.text:
Scans your Steam library folders for HV-PlugNPlay.bat and automatically injects it into the game's Steam Launch Options.

Use this when: - You want to launch a Hypervisor game directly from Steam - The HV launch option is missing from the game in Steam

Windows 11 Only

HV-PlugNPlay.bat only works on Windows 11. Steam will close and restart to apply the changes.

An Injector Terminal will appear showing the injection progress and results.

docs.title: 🛡️ Clear Denuvo Token (Error 88500012)
docs.text:
Fixes the Denuvo activation error 88500012 for a specific game.

What it does: 1. Closes Steam 2. Deletes the AppID's userdata token and OpenSteamTool owner key 3. Restarts Steam and re-applies the fix

How to use: 1. Enter the game's AppID in the input box (e.g. 1245620) 2. Click Clear Token

Finding a game's AppID

The AppID is in the game's Steam store URL: store.steampowered.com/app/**1245620**/Game_Name/

docs.title: ☁️ CloudRedirect Launcher
docs.text:
A third-party tool (not made by ONENNABE) that provides extra features for managing games and saves.

Features: - One Click Run Patch — auto-sets up all necessary patches - Cloud saves — uses your Google account as the cloud save location - CAPCOM game save fixes - Offline play — the Play button stays active even without internet. Do not use Steam's built-in Go Offline mode, or you will need to use Fix Steam Stuck at Loading - Lock game version via Manifest Pinning - Backup achievements, playtime and unlocked games to Google — sign in to the same Google account on another PC to sync everything back

Third-party tool

CloudRedirect is not affiliated with ONENNABE. Download and launch it from within the Tools section.

Full tutorial at CloudRedirect

docs.title: 🗑️ Delete Standalone Cache
docs.text:
Removes the entire Steam\depot_key\ folder, which contains all stored depot keys and bypass files.

Warning

After deleting, games will need to be re-processed before they can be downloaded again. Only use this if instructed to by support or if you are doing a clean reset.

docs.title: 🖼️ Fix Blank Steam Icons
docs.text:
Scans every installed game that has a Desktop shortcut and repairs its icon.

What it does: 1. Downloads the correct icon from Steam CDN (with SteamGridDB as fallback) 2. Saves it to Steam\steam\games\ 3. Repoints the Desktop shortcut to the correct icon

Note

Games without a Desktop shortcut are skipped. Based on SteamIconFix — not affiliated with ONENNABE.

docs.title: 🧹 Clear SUO Cache
docs.text:
Wipes ONENNABE's cached app data — thumbnails, store cache, and temporary files.

Your licence is kept. The app reloads automatically after clearing.

Use this when: - Game covers or thumbnails are not loading correctly - The store is showing outdated or incorrect data

docs.title: 🗑️ Remove All Games
docs.text:
Destructive — Cannot Be Undone

Deletes every unlocked game from your Steam library. You will need to re-add games afterward. Your Steam installation is not affected.

Use this only if you want to completely reset your unlocked game list. Your ONENNABE licence and installed game files are not affected.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: If something is not working — patch not applying, game not launching, button missing — follow these steps in order. Try each fix before moving to the next.

docs.title: Fix 1 — Restart Your PC
docs.text:
The simplest fix and often the most effective.

  1. Restart your PC fully
  2. Let Windows finish booting completely
  3. Open Steam and wait for it to fully load
  4. Open ONENNABE and try again

docs.title: Fix 2 — Fix Unlock Game Issue
docs.text:
1. Open Steam Unlock ONENNABE
  2. Go to the Tools section
  3. Click Fix Now
  4. Wait for it to complete
  5. Steam will restart — log back in
  6. Try again

docs.title: Fix 3 — Clear Steam Download Cache
docs.text:
1. Open Steam Unlock ONENNABE
  2. Go to the Tools section
  3. Click Clear Steam Download Cache
  4. Wait for it to complete
  5. Steam will restart — log back in
  6. Try again

docs.title: Fix 4 — Reinstall Steam (via ONENNABE Tools)
docs.text:
If all the above did not help, use the Reinstall Steam option built into ONENNABE. This does a clean Steam reinstall without affecting your downloaded games. You have to restart Steam Unlock Onennabe once you have complete reinstalling Steam.

  1. Open Steam Unlock ONENNABE
  2. Go to the Tools section
  3. Click Reinstall Steam
  4. Wait for the process to complete
  5. Re-login to Steam with your account credentials after it finishes
  6. Reopen ONENNABE — activate with your CD Key if prompted
  7. Try again

Have Your Login Ready

After reinstalling Steam, you must log back in. Have your Steam username and password ready before starting this step.

docs.title: Still Not Working?
docs.text:
Join the Discord server and post in the support channel. Include:

  • Which game you are having issues with
  • Which fix steps you already tried
  • Any error messages you are seeing

docs.title: Unlocking Games
docs.text: This page covers how to find and unlock a game in ONENNABE and get it ready to download.

docs.title: Step 1 — Find the Game in the Store Tab
docs.text:
1. Open Steam Unlock ONENNABE
  2. Go to the Store tab
  3. Browse or use the search bar to find the game you want
  4. Click the game card to open the Game Detail modal

docs.title: Step 2 — Unlock the Game
docs.text:
Inside the Game Detail modal:

  1. Click Unlock Game
  2. ONENNABE will apply the unlock to your Steam account
  3. The game will now appear in your Steam library

docs.title: Step 3 — Click Smart Apply in Game Auto Update (Before Downloading)
docs.text:
Before downloading in Steam, clicking Smart Apply is recommended to ensure game is either lock version or need an update:

SUO > Tools > Game Auto Update > click Open > Smart Apply

Default state

By default, games are already in Lock mode. Disable = Lock | Enable = Unlock Always click Smart Apply before downloading.

docs.title: Step 4 — Download from Steam
docs.text:
1. Open Steam
  2. Find the game in your library
  3. Click Install and wait for it to fully download

docs.title: ⚠ 32-Hour Cooldown
docs.text:
Applies to Denuvo, Hypervisor, Online Patch & Bypass Games Only

Games that fall under Denuvo, Hypervisor, Online Patch, or Bypass categories are locked behind a 32-hour waiting period after CD Key activation before you can unlock and play them.

  • The 32-hour lock starts from the moment of CD Key activation
  • You will not be able to unlock these games until the 32 hours have passed
  • This is by design and cannot be bypassed or shortened

Regular games are not affected

Games that do not require Denuvo, Hypervisor, Online Patch, or Bypass can be unlocked and played without any cooldown restriction.

docs.title: Using the Search Bar & SteamDB Button
docs.text:
The search bar in the Store tab lets you find games by name or AppID.

Next to the search bar is a SteamDB button — clicking it opens steamdb.info directly in your browser.

docs.title: What is SteamDB?
docs.text:
SteamDB is a third-party site that provides detailed information about every game on Steam, including:

  • AppID — the unique number that identifies a game on Steam
  • Pricing history, DLC list, depot info, and more

docs.title: How to Find a Game's AppID Using SteamDB
docs.text:
1. Click the SteamDB button next to the search bar in ONENNABE
  2. SteamDB opens in your browser
  3. Search for the game by name
  4. The AppID is shown on the game's SteamDB page and also in the URL: 
    https://steamdb.info/app/1245620/
                           ↑↑↑↑↑↑↑
                           This is the AppID

When do you need the AppID?

The AppID is needed when using Clear Denuvo Token in Tools, or when searching for a specific game by its ID in the ONENNABE search bar.

docs.title: Filtering Games in the Store
docs.text: The Store tab has a filter bar at the top with three dropdown filters and a search box to help you find the right game quickly.

docs.title: Games Filter
docs.text:
Click Games ▾ to filter by game category:

Option What It Shows Denuvo Games Only games with Denuvo DRM Top 20 Downloads The 20 most downloaded games in the catalogue

docs.title: Features Filter
docs.text:
Click Features ▾ to filter by game type:

Option What It Shows Online Supported Games that have an online patch available Bypass Available Games that have a bypass patch available ⚙️ Hypervisor Bypass Games that require the Hypervisor bypass method

Tip

You can combine multiple feature filters at once — for example, select both Online Supported and Bypass Available to show games that have either tag.

docs.title: Genre Filter
docs.text: Click Genre ▾ to filter games by genre (e.g. Action, RPG, Sports, Strategy). Genre chips are populated automatically from the game catalogue. Click any genre chip to show only games in that genre.

docs.title: Clearing Filters
docs.text:
Click the ✕ Clear button in the filter bar to remove all active filters and return to the full catalogue view.

A coloured dot appears on any filter button that has an active selection, so you can quickly see which filters are on.

docs.title: Still Not Working?
docs.text: See the Troubleshooting page for step-by-step fixes.

docs.title: Updating Games
docs.text: This page explains how to manage game updates in ONENNABE — both through the Game Auto Update tool and the UPDATE GAME button in the Library tab.

docs.title: Why Game Updates Need to Be Managed
docs.text: Steam automatically updates games by default. For most games this is fine, but for games with Bypass or Hypervisor patches, a Steam update will overwrite the patched files and break the game. ONENNABE lets you control which games update automatically and which ones stay locked.

docs.title: How to Open
docs.text:
ONENNABE > Tools > Game Auto Update > click Open

This opens the Game Auto Update modal showing every unlocked game in your library with its current update setting.

docs.title: Update Settings
docs.text:
Each game can be set to one of two states:

Setting What It Means Auto Update (green) Steam will update the game automatically when a new version is available Locked (red) Steam will not update the game — it stays on its current version

docs.title: Recommended Settings per Game Type
docs.text:
ONENNABE uses this logic to determine the correct setting for each game:

Game Type Recommended Setting Reason No special tags ✅ Auto Update Safe to update — no patches to protect Denuvo Protected ✅ Auto Update Must stay updated for Denuvo to work Bypass Available 🔒 Lock Updates will overwrite bypass files Hypervisor Bypass 🔒 Lock Updates will overwrite HV patch files

docs.title: ⚡ Smart Apply
docs.text:
Smart Apply automatically applies the correct setting to every game that is currently mismatched.

How to use:

  1. Open Tools → Game Auto Update → Open
  2. Click ⚡ Smart Apply
  3. ONENNABE scans all your games and compares their current setting against the recommended setting
  4. Any mismatched games are corrected automatically
  5. A summary shows how many games were corrected

Run Smart Apply before downloading any game

Always run Smart Apply before downloading a new Bypass or Hypervisor game to make sure the version gets locked correctly before Steam can update it.

Already correctly set

If all games are already on the correct setting, Smart Apply will show "All games are already correctly set. No changes needed."

docs.title: Other Buttons in Game Auto Update
docs.text:
Button What It Does All On Sets every game to Auto Update All Off Sets every game to Locked Rescan Refreshes the game list from your lua folder

Warning

Use All On and All Off with caution — they override every game at once without checking game type. Use ⚡ Smart Apply instead for the correct per-game setting.

docs.title: Method 2 — UPDATE GAME Button (Library Tab)
docs.text: The ⬆ UPDATE GAME button redownloads the game's manifest directly from the ONENNABE GitHub server. It does not update the game through Steam.

docs.title: When to Use UPDATE GAME
docs.text:
This button is used in specific situations — it is not for routine Steam game updates.

Situation Why UPDATE GAME Helps "No internet connection" error in Steam Redownloads the manifest to refresh Steam's connection to the game — this is a rare case "Content still encrypted" error The ONENNABE developer has pushed a fix to the server — UPDATE GAME pulls the latest version New DLC added to the game The developer has updated the .lua file with new DLC entries — UPDATE GAME pulls the updated file

docs.title: Important — Game Returns to Locked Version
docs.text:
Game version will be reset to locked

After clicking UPDATE GAME, the game manifest is redownloaded from the server and the game version is returned to its locked state. This means:

  • The game may show as needing an update in Steam
  • You must run ⚡ Smart Apply again after using UPDATE GAME to reapply the correct update settings

docs.title: Step-by-Step
docs.text:
1. Go to the Library tab in ONENNABE
  2. Find the game and click ⬆ UPDATE GAME
  3. Wait for the manifest to finish downloading from the server
  4. Once complete, go to Tools → Game Auto Update → Open → click ⚡ Smart Apply
  5. Launch the game as normal

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Situation What to Do Setting up for the first time Run ⚡ Smart Apply in Tools → Game Auto Update Before downloading a Bypass or HV game Run ⚡ Smart Apply to lock the version first "No internet connection" error in Steam Click ⬆ UPDATE GAME, then run ⚡ Smart Apply again "Content still encrypted" error Click ⬆ UPDATE GAME, then run ⚡ Smart Apply again New DLC added to the game Click ⬆ UPDATE GAME, then run ⚡ Smart Apply again Want to manually control all games at once Use All On or All Off in Game Auto Update

docs.title: Still Not Working?
docs.text: See the Troubleshooting page for step-by-step fixes.
